title: Economic
summary: 9609 A Level — recession, inflation, interest rates, exchange rates, and business impact.
learningObjectives: Explain effects of economic growth and recession.
Analyse inflation and interest rate changes.
Evaluate exchange rate impact on trade.
Apply economic PESTLE to case decisions.

simpleExplanation.title: The economic weather
simpleExplanation.summary: Macroeconomic conditions affect demand, costs, and financing — recessions hit sales; inflation raises costs; interest rates affect loans; exchange rates shift export competitiveness.
steps: Recession reduces consumer and business spending.
Inflation raises wages and material costs.
Higher interest rates increase loan repayments.
Currency moves affect exporters and importers.

flashcards.back: Falling demand, unemployment rises, consumers trade down, B2B orders fall.
Higher input costs; need to raise prices or accept lower margin; wage pressure.
Higher cost of borrowing; reduced consumer credit purchases; mortgage pressure reduces discretionary spend.
Exports more expensive abroad — volume may fall unless quality dominates.
Imported raw materials cost more — margin squeeze or price rises.
Interest rates affect choice and cost of debt; recession affects cash flow.

sections.content: **Economic** factors (6.1.2) appear in almost every A Level case. Link **macro change → specific impact on case firm** (demand, costs, finance).

sections.question: Country in recession: GDP −2%, unemployment rising. A mid-range fashion retailer analyses two impacts and one response.
sections.solution:
**Impact 1 — Demand:** Consumers **cut discretionary spending** — fewer new outfits; shift to **value/discount** rivals.

**Impact 2 — Costs:** May still face **sticky rent and wages** while revenue falls — **profit squeeze**.

**Response:** **Reposition mix** toward basics and promotions (3.3); **reduce inventory** orders; negotiate **rent holidays**; delay **store expansion** (10.3 investment).
